message:
chore(beads): close br-r37-c1-06ubx (dag_to_branching multigraph guard)

Fixed in commit 019ebd0. dag_to_branching now also rejects
MultiDiGraph input matching nx's stacked
@not_implemented_for('multigraph') decorator. Decorator-ordering
parity preserved: MultiGraph yields 'not implemented for undirected
type', MultiDiGraph yields 'not implemented for multigraph type'.

Sweep: 6720 passed, 0 failed (+2 new regression tests).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -2,7 +2,7 @@
 {"id":"br-r37-c1-01","title":"REVIEW: [HIGH] condensation used wrong SCC labeling (source-first instead of sink-first)","description":"fnx.condensation(G) labeled SCCs via the Rust _condensation_raw internal order, which numbered source SCCs first. nx numbers SCCs in strongly_connected_components() order (sinks first). Consequence: C.graph['mapping'], C.nodes[i]['members'], and condensation edges had integer labels flipped relative to networkx, silently breaking downstream code that indexes SCCs by label. Fixed by rebuilding condensation on top of fnx's own strongly_connected_components iterator so labels stay bit-compatible.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-24T00:00:00Z","updated_at":"2026-04-24T00:00:00Z","closed_at":"2026-04-24T00:00:00Z","close_reason":"Fixed: condensation now iterates strongly_connected_components(G) and assigns indices in emission order, matching networkx's convention. Tested on 5-node 2-SCC DAG; mapping, edges, and members all match nx exactly; scc= arg path and empty-graph path also match.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"br-r37-c1-02","title":"REVIEW: [MEDIUM] node_connectivity on MultiGraph with parallel edges returned simple-graph value","description":"fnx.node_connectivity(MultiGraph) called into _raw_node_connectivity which collapses parallel edges. On e.g. K3 with extra parallel edges between two pairs, networkx returns min-degree=3 (parallel edges inflate the degree bound) while fnx returned 2 (simple-graph κ=n−1). Fixed by delegating multigraph inputs to networkx to preserve parity. Simple Graph/DiGraph continue to use the Rust fast path.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-24T00:00:00Z","updated_at":"2026-04-24T00:00:00Z","closed_at":"2026-04-24T00:00:00Z","close_reason":"Fixed: node_connectivity now delegates when G.is_multigraph(). Verified on K3-parallel, K3 MG no-parallel, MDG with parallel edges — all match nx.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"br-r37-c1-03","title":"REVIEW: [MEDIUM] edge_connectivity returned float and ignored MultiGraph parallel edges","description":"Two related defects in edge_connectivity: (1) simple Graph path returned a float (e.g. 2.0) via the max-flow pipeline while nx returns an int cardinality (2). (2) MultiGraph path collapsed parallel edges: on K3 with extra parallel edges fnx returned 2.0 vs nx's 3. Fixed by coercing int-valued floats to int before returning, and delegating multigraph inputs to networkx.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-24T00:00:00Z","updated_at":"2026-04-24T00:00:00Z","closed_at":"2026-04-24T00:00:00Z","close_reason":"Fixed: MG/MDG delegate to nx; simple Graph path casts integral float back to int. Verified across K3 simple, DG, MG with/without parallel, MDG — all match nx in value and type.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id":"br-r37-c1-06ubx","title":"dag_to_branching missing @not_implemented_for('multigraph') guard","description":"Follow-up to br-r37-c1-6nhsw. nx.dag_to_branching is decorated with BOTH @not_implemented_for('multigraph') AND @not_implemented_for('undirected'). The previous fix added the undirected guard but missed the multigraph one.

Repro:
  >>> g = fnx.MultiDiGraph([(1, 2), (2, 3)])
  >>> fnx.dag_to_branching(g)        # silently succeeds
  >>> nx.dag_to_branching(nx.MultiDiGraph([(1, 2), (2, 3)]))
  NetworkXNotImplemented: not implemented for multigraph type

Fix: add @not_implemented_for('multigraph') equivalent guard before the existing undirected/cycle checks.","status":"in_progress","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-27T07:40:14.114541567Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-27T07:40:45.075833132Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"br-r37-c1-06ubx","title":"dag_to_branching missing @not_implemented_for('multigraph') guard","description":"Follow-up to br-r37-c1-6nhsw. nx.dag_to_branching is decorated with BOTH @not_implemented_for('multigraph') AND @not_implemented_for('undirected'). The previous fix added the undirected guard but missed the multigraph one.

Repro:
  >>> g = fnx.MultiDiGraph([(1, 2), (2, 3)])
  >>> fnx.dag_to_branching(g)        # silently succeeds
  >>> nx.dag_to_branching(nx.MultiDiGraph([(1, 2), (2, 3)]))
  NetworkXNotImplemented: not implemented for multigraph type

Fix: add @not_implemented_for('multigraph') equivalent guard before the existing undirected/cycle checks.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-27T07:40:14.114541567Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-27T07:45:58.154883400Z","closed_at":"2026-04-27T07:45:58.152042700Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"br-r37-c1-0l76i","title":"dijkstra_predecessor_and_distance pred dict iteration order drifts from nx","description":"fnx.dijkstra_predecessor_and_distance returns the predecessor dict with keys in distances iteration order, but nx returns it in insertion order during traversal (which differs because pred entries are populated when an edge relaxation finds a tentative path, not when the node is finalized).

Repro:
  edges = [(a,b,1),(b,c,2),(c,d,1),(a,d,5),(b,d,3)] (weighted)
  fnx.dijkstra_predecessor_and_distance(g, 'a')[0] -> {'a':[], 'b':['a'], 'c':['b'], 'd':['b','c']}
  nx .dijkstra_predecessor_and_distance(g, 'a')[0]  -> {'a':[], 'b':['a'], 'd':['b','c'], 'c':['b']}

The values are correct; only key order differs. nx populates pred[d] with 'b' when edge (b,d) is relaxed (before c is popped), then appends 'c' when (c,d) ties — so 'd' lands in the dict before 'c'.

Drop-in code that iterated the predecessor dict in nx's algorithm-discovery order broke. Fix: delegate dijkstra_predecessor_and_distance to nx (the local _single_source_dijkstra_path_basic_local helper initialises {node:[] for node in G} and would need a per-call adapter to track pred-insertion order).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T18:40:08.669610779Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T18:42:54.081452293Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T18:42:54.079229614Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"br-r37-c1-0zdk4","title":"parity: is_minimal_d_separator missing kwargs + generate_random_paths weight default mismatch","description":"Two remaining real signature defects: (1) is_minimal_d_separator(G, x, y, z) — fnx is missing the keyword-only included=None, restricted=None kwargs that nx accepts; users who pass them get TypeError. (2) generate_random_paths(G, sample_size, ..., weight=None, ...) — fnx default is None but nx default is weight, which causes sampled paths to ignore weights by default in fnx. Fix is_minimal_d_separator by routing through nx via _call_networkx_for_parity (mirrors find_minimal_d_separator fix in br-71glx). Fix generate_random_paths by changing weight default to weight to match nx.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T03:42:48.730698907Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T03:54:46.925463070Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T03:54:46.923617266Z","close_reason":"Verified already-fixed in main: is_minimal_d_separator now has (G, x, y, z, *, included=None, restricted=None) matching nx; generate_random_paths weight default is now weight matching nx. Both must have been aligned by another agent or earlier session commit between probe runs.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"br-r37-c1-14pu1","title":"MultiGraph/MultiDiGraph constructor mishandles dict-of-lists and 3-level dict-of-dicts","description":"Two defects in fnx.MultiGraph and fnx.MultiDiGraph constructor parity with nx:

1) dict-of-lists symmetric input duplicates edges. For input {1:[2,3], 2:[1,3], 3:[1,2]} (the symmetric adjacency-list representation of K3), nx returns 3 edges via the 'seen-node' dedupe in nx.convert.from_dict_of_lists (which only adds (u,v) when v has not yet been processed). fnx adds all 6 edges. Graph (non-multi) is unaffected because Graph dedupes structurally.

2) dict-of-dicts 3-level input misuses inner dict. For input {1: {2: {'w': 1}}}, nx reads this as 'edge (1,2) with attr w=1' (auto-key 0). fnx instead reads it as 'edge (1,2) with key=w' (no attrs). nx detects 3-level vs 4-level by checking whether the inner-most dict's values are dicts; fnx always assumes 4-level for MultiGraph.

Both regressions affect drop-in compat for code that uses these constructors.

Repro:
  >>> nx.MultiGraph({1:[2,3], 2:[1,3], 3:[1,2]}).number_of_edges()  # 3
  >>> fnx.MultiGraph({1:[2,3], 2:[1,3], 3:[1,2]}).number_of_edges()  # 6
  >>> list(nx.MultiGraph({1:{2:{'w':1}}}).edges(keys=True, data=True))  # [(1,2,0,{'w':1})]
  >>> list(fnx.MultiGraph({1:{2:{'w':1}}}).edges(keys=True, data=True))  # [(1,2,'w',{})]","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T22:56:40.696205272Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T23:08:09.897126157Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T23:08:09.894444627Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
